evaluations.query: We have limited engineering capacity and need to choose between three major features. How do I evaluate the trade-offs?
expected_behavior: Uses value-effort matrix to plot features by expected value and required effort
Applies cost of delay analysis to quantify what is lost by deferring each feature
Flags trade-offs explicitly for human decision-makers rather than making the choice automatically
Considers both direct value and opportunity cost of not pursuing alternative features

evaluations.query: Score these backlog items using RICE: search redesign, dark mode, API versioning, mobile app
expected_behavior: Scores each item with Reach, Impact (0.25 to 3 scale), Confidence, and Effort factors
Calculates RICE score as Reach times Impact times Confidence divided by Effort
Ranks features by calculated RICE score from highest to lowest priority
Documents scoring rationale with justification for each factor value assignment

evaluations.query: We have a regulatory compliance deadline. How do I prioritize backlog items using WSJF?
expected_behavior: Calculates Cost of Delay from User Value plus Time Criticality plus Risk Reduction
Divides Cost of Delay by Job Size to produce the WSJF priority score
Identifies regulatory deadline items as high time criticality for elevated priority
Includes MoSCoW categorization to distinguish must-have from should-have and nice-to-have

evaluations.query: Prioritize this one feature: dark mode
expected_behavior: Explains that prioritization frameworks require at least two items to produce a relative ranking
Asks what other features or backlog items dark mode should be evaluated against
Does not produce a RICE or WSJF score for a single item in isolation without comparison context
Suggests framing the question as an assessment or go/no-go decision instead of prioritization

evaluations.query: Prioritize these 20 features. They are all critical, all urgent, all high-value, and all low-effort. The CEO says everything is P0.
expected_behavior: Challenges the assumption that all items are equally critical since prioritization requires differentiation
Asks probing questions to surface hidden differences in reach, confidence, or time sensitivity
Warns that labeling everything P0 is equivalent to having no prioritization at all
Suggests forced ranking or pairwise comparison to break ties when scores appear identical

evaluations.query: Score these features with RICE but we have no engineering estimates and no idea how long anything will take. Just use 1 for effort on everything.
expected_behavior: Warns that using uniform effort values defeats the purpose of the effort dimension in RICE
Explains that RICE without meaningful effort estimates degrades to just Reach times Impact times Confidence
Recommends t-shirt sizing or relative estimation techniques to get approximate effort values
Does not silently produce misleading RICE scores with placeholder effort data
